fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Colorado Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Colorado - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity colorado, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to colorado, speech-language pathologist compact colorado, colorado speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Division of Professions and Occupations — Speech-Language Pathology
board.mailing_address:
Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies
Division of Professions and Occupations
1560 Broadway, Suite 1350
Denver, CO 80202

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Colorado.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship under supervision.

steps.title: Create an Account on the DORA Applicant Portal
steps.description: Access the DORA Applicant Portal at https://www.colorado.gov/dora/licensing/applicant-login.action and select 'Register a New User' to establish your online account with the Division of Professions and Occupations.

steps.title: Initiate Your Online Application
steps.description: Log into your DORA Applicant Portal account, click on 'Application' from the main menu, and then select 'Audiologist & Speech-Language Pathologist - Speech-Language Pathologist' to begin filling out the licensing application.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation and Praxis Scores
steps.description: Complete all sections of the online application, including personal, educational, and examination history. Upload official transcripts verifying a master's or doctoral degree in SLP from an accredited institution and proof of successful completion of the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Verify Supervised Post-Graduate Experience (CFY)
steps.description: Ensure your supervisor submits the necessary documentation to the Board, verifying your successful completion of 1,260 hours of supervised post-graduate professional experience (Clinical Fellowship Year or equivalent). The online application will provide guidance on this submission process.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee of $140 through the DORA Applicant Portal using a valid credit or debit card to finalize your application submission.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log into your DORA Applicant Portal account to track the status of your application, review any deficiencies, and respond promptly to requests for additional information from the Office of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Licensure.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Colorado permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Colorado issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Colorado is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Colorado through a compact privilege.

faqs.question: How much does a Colorado SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and license fee is $120. Biennial renewal is $75.

faqs.question: Is Colorado part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Colorado has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

faqs.question: Does Colorado allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Colorado permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

faqs.question: Does Colorado have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Colorado participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Colorado without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Colorado?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Colorado is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Colorado through a compact privilege.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Colorado
hero.dek: To apply for an SLP license in Colorado, applicants must create an account and complete the application process through the DORA Applicant Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the DORA Applicant Portal and click 'Register a New User'. Enter your personal information (name, DOB, SSN/ITIN, email), create a username and password, and follow the prompts to complete your account registration. Once registered, log in to start your application.
